houseRules.turnLoop: narrate -> resolve via engine -> reconcile state

inventory.note: Iron seal of the Vellmark Trading Company. The only thing left of the convoy.

inventory[5].note: Druid-gifted disc worn as amulet. Advantage on WIS saves vs charm and domination effects.

vector.goal: Expose the conspiracy publicly — sunlight, not a quiet deal or a body in a ditch
vector.secret: The threshold has an informant in Crale's market committee; she suspects who but can't prove it
vector.voice: Direct, hands busy when nervous, asks before she gives anything
vector.attitude: committed ally (conditional) — won over by Aldric's free, corroborating account of the silver-eyed convoy and the cloaked man; allied on one hard term: all findings go to Crale's public committee, no back-room deals

vector.goal: Know whether her husband's death was an accident — and what the ledger means
vector.secret: She gave a 'company auditor' full access to Caspar's workshop records; they were threshold agents erasing traces
vector.voice: Soft-spoken, deflects with hospitality, becomes precise and careful when she commits to a statement
vector.attitude: fearful-and-desperate — has been watched since the auditor visit; needs protection but doesn't understand from what

vector.goal: Disappear cleanly — will trade information for a head start and a closed mouth
vector.secret: He specified the Hollow Road route for Aldric's convoy on a separate payment; doesn't know why Aldric survived
vector.voice: Charming, self-deprecating humor, never holds eye contact when Vellmark comes up
vector.attitude: transactional — sees Aldric as a threat he might be able to make useful

vector.goal: Help patients without becoming a target — caught between duty and the survival instinct
vector.secret: Has been reporting unusual injuries to a civic contact he trusts; that contact may be compromised
vector.voice: Professorial, reaches for medical terminology, genuinely curious about anomalies, uses clinical distance to deflect alarm
vector.attitude: professionally-helpful, growing alarmed — already deeper in this than he intended

vector.goal: Protect his family and disappear from this — he recanted because threshold men visited his wife
vector.secret: He saw threshold agents loading people (not goods) from Greywater Crossing dock the night of the attack
vector.voice: Short sentences, answers the literal question, never volunteers
vector.attitude: closed-by-fear — will only talk if silence feels more dangerous than speaking

what-happened-on-the-road.objective: Find out what destroyed the Vellmark convoy on the Hollow Road
what-happened-on-the-road.clue: Aldric is the only survivor and doesn't remember the final hour
what-happened-on-the-road.stake: Truth, and whatever is still out there on the road

vellmark-trading-company.name: Vellmark Trading Company
vellmark-trading-company.note: Aldric's convoy belonged to them; their fate and Aldric's debt to them are unresolved. Score starts neutral — they haven't moved against him yet, but Corvin's payment trail leads here.

threshold.name: The Threshold
threshold.note: Directed operation behind the convoy enslavement. Aldric's memory recovery attempt triggered detection — he is now actively hunted.

crale-community.name: Crale (town)
crale-community.note: Aldric is a stranger. Trust is earned by action, lost by drawing trouble to their doors.

the-missing-hour.intro: Aldric woke at Greywater Crossing with no memory of how the convoy fell
clues: Iron company token in his hand
No bodies found at the wreck
Recovered memory (Wendel's draught): the convoy guards bore the wolf's silver eyes — enchanted, not slain
They followed a hooded man in a black cloak of their own will — 'no bodies' is abduction by domination, not death
Aldric alone was left frozen, alive, and unmarked
the-missing-hour.hidden: What took the convoy, and why Aldric was spared
the-missing-hour.clues[5]: Holt corroborates: 16 people loaded onto a covered wagon at south dock of Greywater Crossing, silver-eyed, walking willingly
the-missing-hour.clues[6]: Wagon marked with a door-frame brand (two uprights and a lintel) — the Threshold sigil
the-missing-hour.clues[7]: The cloaked figure wore a heavy silver ring with a flat dark-red stone
the-missing-hour.clues[8]: Convoy traveled north on the mill road after loading
the-missing-hour.clues[9]: Aldric was NOT among the 16 loaded — left behind deliberately; reason unknown

buffer.text: Session 2: Recruited Sera as conditional ally (public record only). Holt gave full testimony at Spit and Timber — 16 convoy workers taken alive, silver-eyed, north on mill road, Threshold wagon brand, cloaked figure with dark-red ring. Aldric was deliberately left behind. Investigation phase complete — moving to action.

Session 2 close: Assaulted Ashfeld relay station with Sera — 4 guards down, 15 convoy survivors freed, contractor Renn captured. Convoy was dominated not killed; traveled north on mill road. Fifth guard rode ahead to Crale — intercepted via Wendel contact rider. Aldric rode ahead, broke up Threshold extraction at Mira Voss house, subdued two agents, recovered Caspar Voss ledger intact. Mira unharmed. All three threads now active: Renn in custody, ledger secured, survivors en route to Crale.

log.summary: Session 1 complete: convoy mystery established, threshold identified as directed operation, three leads open (Sera, Corvin, the clearing)
